content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

January 20, 1993

Sara Myers
100 Baldwin Lake Circle
Folsom, CA 95630

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-92-663

Dear Ms. Myers:

This is in response to your request for confirmation of
telephone advice provided to you on December 29, 1992 concerning
the propriety of using campaign funds to purchase a cellular
telephone with campaign funds under the "personal use" provisions
of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

This letter confirms that your letter dated December 29,
1992, accurately summarizes the telephone advice I provided to you
on that date. As we discussed, Section 89517 permits campaign
funds to be used for the purchase of equipment so long as (1) the
legal title of the equipment is in the name of the committee, and
(2) any other use is only incidental to its use for political,
legislative, or governmental purposes.²

Additionally, I advised you that since the cellular telephone
would be used for officeholder purposes, any payments for the
cellular telephone should be made either from the campaign bank
account established for your current term of office or a campaign
account established for a future election (Regulation 18525) and
should be disclosed on your campaign reports. Moreover, you may

___________________________
¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. Sections 85800-
85807, which describe the permissible use of campaign funds, are
referred to as the "personal use" laws.

² Regulation 18951 clarifies that the use of appliances for
personal purposes is incidental if the use occurs in conjunction
with its use for a political, legislative, or governmental purpose
and constitutes only 5 percent or less of the total use of the
item in any one calendar month with a value of less than $100.

428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento, CA 95812 ● (916) 322-5660

Our File No. A-92-663
Page 2


contribute personal funds to your committee in order to pay for
the telephone, so long as the money is deposited into the campaign
bank account before it is expended and both the contribution and
expenditure are fully disclosed.

If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Counsel, Legal Division

sections.question_synthetic: May campaign funds be used to purchase a cellular telephone, and if so, what conditions and disclosure requirements apply under the Political Reform Act's personal use provisions?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Campaign funds may be used to purchase a cellular telephone if legal title is held by the committee and any other use is only incidental to political/legislative/governmental purposes. Payments must be made from a campaign account for the current term or future election and fully disclosed on campaign reports. Alternatively, personal funds may be contributed to the committee to pay for the telephone if properly deposited and disclosed.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a confirmation letter of prior telephone advice dated December 29, 1992. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections exist, but the legal issue and FPPC's position are clearly stated in the body of the letter. Document references Government Code Sections 81000-91015 and Regulation 18951 regarding incidental personal use thresholds.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: May campaign funds be used to purchase a cellular telephone, and if so, what conditions and disclosure requirements apply under the Political Reform Act's personal use provisions?

CONCLUSION: Campaign funds may be used to purchase a cellular telephone if legal title is held by the committee and any other use is only incidental to political/legislative/governmental purposes. Payments must be made from a campaign account for the current term or future election and fully disclosed on campaign reports. Alternatively, personal funds may be contributed to the committee to pay for the telephone if properly deposited and disclosed.

embedding.summary: The FPPC confirms telephone advice regarding the permissibility of using campaign funds to purchase a cellular telephone under Section 89517, subject to specific conditions regarding title ownership, incidental use limitations, and required disclosure on campaign reports.
